refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation link availability at or above the customer-defined target throughout the operational period.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (link availability) and ties it explicitly to the customer-defined target and operational timeframe, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure link uptime and downtime over the evaluation period using telemetry logs and compare against the customer-defined availability target.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor network link status continuously to detect availability.
FR2: Manage network resources to maintain link availability.
FR3: Provide failover or recovery mechanisms to restore link promptly after failure.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement redundant network links with automatic failover.
DP2: Use real-time link health monitoring and adaptive resource allocation.
DP3: Integrate predictive maintenance and fault detection algorithms.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement redundant network links with automatic failover.

potential_sources: Dependency on network infrastructure managed by Teleoperation module.
Interaction with Safety Services for failover alerts.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates link availability management but requires well-defined interfaces to Teleoperation and Safety Services to avoid tight coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes redundant network hardware is available and reliable.
Risk of increased system complexity leading to integration challenges.
Assumes failover mechanisms do not introduce unacceptable latency.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding monitoring and predictive maintenance DPs in future refinement levels to enhance robustness.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Responsible for managing teleoperation link infrastructure, including redundancy and failover mechanisms.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connections managed by Teleoperation for link availability.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Provides real-time link status data to NGHE Solution and Safety Services.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends alerts to Safety Services upon link failure or degradation.

module_containment_scores.notes: High-level coordination but not direct link management.
Limited involvement in network link redundancy.
Primary module responsible for link availability management.
Receives alerts but does not manage link directly.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning link availability DP to Teleoperation module centralizes responsibility and minimizes diffusion, enabling clear ownership and interface management.
containment_risks: Potential overload of Teleoperation module with network management responsibilities.
Interface dependencies with Safety Services must be carefully managed to avoid coupling.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Review interface definitions and consider splitting monitoring functions if Teleoperation module scope grows too large.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (redundant network links with failover) directly addresses the refined FR and is logically sound.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion risk exists if monitoring and alerting functions spread across multiple modules.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows strong containment for the DP with well-defined external interfaces.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification via telemetry and alert logs is feasible within the assigned IM boundary.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility to Teleoperation module with explicit interface usage.

recommended_revisions: Clarify interface protocols between Teleoperation and Safety Services for alerting.
Consider future refinement to separate monitoring and predictive maintenance.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, further refinement is needed to explicitly address monitoring and recovery sub-functions and to clarify interface protocols.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor network link status continuously to detect availability.
FR2: Manage network resources to maintain link availability.
FR3: Provide failover or recovery mechanisms to restore link promptly after failure.

revise.why: To decompose the high-level FR into atomic sub-FRs that enable more precise DP and IM assignments.

assumptions: Redundant network hardware is available and reliable.
Teleoperation module can handle additional network management responsibilities.
open_risks: Potential complexity and coupling with Safety Services.
Verification of failover latency within customer-defined targets.
evidence_needed: Telemetry data demonstrating link availability performance.
Test results of failover mechanisms under operational conditions.
